sn47.35:1.3: This is our instruction to you all.
sn47.35:2.2: There is the case where a monk remains focused on the body in & of itself—ardent, alert, & mindful—subduing greed & distress with reference to the world.
sn47.35:2.5: mental qualities in & of themselves—ardent, alert, & mindful—subduing greed & distress with reference to the world.
sn47.35:2.6: This is how a monk is mindful.
sn47.35:3.2: There is the case where feelings are known to the monk as they arise, known as they persist, known as they subside.
sn47.35:3.3: Thoughts are known to him as they arise, known as they persist, known as they subside.
sn47.35:3.4: Perceptions are known to him as they arise, known as they persist, known as they subside.
sn47.35:3.5: This is how a monk is alert.
sn47.35:3.7: This is our instruction to you all.”
